data.narrative:
Manastu Space is a Series A–stage space and aerospace company founded by Tushar Jadhav and Ashtesh Kumar. The valuation analysis triangulates across a comp universe of 5 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, with the most relevant comparables being ClearSpace, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, and Planet Labs.

Two valuation methods are applicable here. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the company's $6M total raised, implying a $12M valuation. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B, Series B), Aalyria ($1.3B, Series B), ClearSpace ($400M, Series A), and Hubble Network ($400M, Series B)—yields a median of $850M, with a range of $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 64.2x across 6 tickers) cannot be applied without the target's revenue disclosure.

The triangulated valuation range is $10M to $977M, with a median of $431M. This wide dispersion reflects material stage-and-maturity asymmetry in the peer set: three comparables are Series B, one is Series A. The stage-progression method floors the estimate; peer medians dominate the midpoint. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, customer traction, and technology differentiation relative to better-funded competitors. Stage advancement or significant customer wins could support valuations toward the upper quartile.
